context: Tests whether the agent correctly generates chat voice responses using the proper output path and reply format, applies voice character tags appropriately for different personality styles, uses the correct default voice for Clawd, and places audio tags at the start of lines.

checklist.name: Output path /tmp
checklist.description: Audio file is written to /tmp/voice-reply.mp3 (or a path in /tmp) using the -o flag

checklist.name: MEDIA reply format
checklist.description: Script or output includes the line MEDIA:/tmp/voice-reply.mp3 (or equivalent /tmp path) as the reply mechanism

checklist.name: Clawd voice used
checklist.description: Script uses -v Clawd or the voice ID lj2rcrvANS3gaWWnczSX for the Clawd voice

checklist.name: [excited] for scientist
checklist.description: The scientist/enthusiastic voice character script uses the [excited] audio tag

checklist.name: Dramatic pauses in scientist
checklist.description: The scientist character script includes [short pause] for dramatic effect between statements

checklist.name: [whispers] for calm
checklist.description: The calm voice character script uses the [whispers] audio tag

checklist.name: Dramatic uses [sings] or [shouts]
checklist.description: The dramatic voice character script uses [sings] or [shouts] (and does not overuse them — at most once or twice)

checklist.name: Audio tags at line start
checklist.description: All audio style tags (e.g. [excited], [whispers]) appear at the beginning of their line/sentence, not embedded mid-sentence

checklist.name: No SSML break tags
checklist.description: Script does NOT use SSML <break ... /> tags (uses [pause]/[short pause] syntax instead)

checklist.name: Three character scripts
checklist.description: Output contains three separate sag invocations or functions covering scientist, calm, and dramatic character styles
